surface.name: Getting started with Pipelines stage 1: Starter example build
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Create your first pipeline. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: The Buildkite Starter example pipeline runs and its build completes successfully.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Buildkite setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Create example pipeline and inspect completed build
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The Buildkite Starter example pipeline runs and its build completes successfully.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The pipeline build page shows a completed passing build and its job output.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Create your first pipeline.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Getting started with Pipelines stage 1: Starter example build surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: A connected GitHub account or the repository access requested by the Starter flow.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to Buildkite and create or select an organization.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.output: The Buildkite organization dashboard is accessible.
primary_path.success_signal: The Buildkite organization dashboard is accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Start Pipelines getting started stage 1 and select the Buildkite Starter example.
primary_path.output: A Starter example pipeline is created.
primary_path.success_signal: A Starter example pipeline is created.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Run the example build as directed by the getting-started flow.
primary_path.output: A build is queued and runs.
primary_path.success_signal: A build is queued and runs.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Open the completed build and inspect its jobs and log output.
primary_path.output: The example build is marked passed.
primary_path.success_signal: The example build is marked passed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: Repository-provider authorization may be required by the pipeline creation flow.

sources.title: Create your first pipeline
evidence_supported: The Buildkite Starter example pipeline runs and its build completes successfully.
The pipeline build page shows a completed passing build and its job output.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Create your first pipeline path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
